architecture_summary: The paper proposes a Frozen Pretrained Transformer (FPT) for time series analysis, reusing frozen self-attention and feedforward layers from pre-trained language or image models while fine-tuning the input embedding, normalization layers, and output layer. It applies patching and reverse instance normalization to adapt the pretrained backbone to classification, anomaly detection, imputation, forecasting, few-shot, and zero-shot time series tasks.

novel_contributions: We propose a unified framework that uses a frozen pre-trained language model to achieve a SOTA or comparable performance in all major types of time series analysis tasks supported by thorough and extensive experiments, including time series classification, short/long-term forecasting, imputation, anomaly detection, few-shot and zero-sample forecasting.
We found, both theoretically and empirically, that self-attention performs a function similar to PCA, which helps explain the universality of transformer models.
We demonstrate the universality of our approach by exploring a pre-trained transformer model from another backbone model (BERT) or modality (computer vision) to power the time series forecasting.

key_claims.claim: The paper proposes a unified framework that uses a frozen pre-trained language model for general time series analysis across classification, anomaly detection, forecasting, and few-shot/zero-shot learning.
GPT2-backbone FPT outperforms other models in most tasks, including long/short-term forecasting, classification, anomaly detection, imputation, and few-shot/zero-shot forecasting.
GPT2(3) FPT achieves the best performance on most imputation datasets and yields a relative 11.5% MSE reduction on ETT1 versus TimesNet.
GPT2(6) FPT achieves an average accuracy of 74.00% on UEA classification, surpassing TimesNet (73.60%).
GPT2(6) FPT achieves the best averaged F1-score of 86.72% on anomaly detection and surpasses TimesNet by 1.7%.
GPT2(6) FPT achieves comparable performance with PatchTST on long-term forecasting and yields a relative 9.3% average MSE reduction versus TimesNet.
GPT2(6) FPT consistently outperforms recent methods in zero-shot forecasting and is comparable to N-BEATS without meta-learning design.
The paper argues both theoretically and empirically that self-attention behaves similarly to PCA, and that this helps explain transformer universality.
key_claims.evidence_section: 8 Towards Understanding the Universality of Transformer: Connecting Self-Attention with PCA
